methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Zeromark is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Joel Anderson that has raised $10M+. Valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public frontier-tech peers in adjacent defense and security segments, including Series A-stage companies Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M), alongside Seed-stage peer Tenna Systems ($60M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $10M raised as a baseline proxy and applies a typical 2.5x multiplier for Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $25M valuation. Peer-Median analysis across the four same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $225M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. The wide dispersion reflects the heterogeneity typical of early-stage defense companies at varying growth trajectories.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $21M (low) to $259M (high), with a median estimate of $125M. The gap between stage-progression ($25M) and peer-median ($225M) reflects a fundamental tension: early-stage multiples from raised capital suggest modest near-term value, while peer comparables—dominated by later-stage companies—imply substantially higher valuations. Key uncertainties include the company's technology differentiation within the peer set, capital efficiency trajectory, and customer traction relative to Series A-stage comparables.
